perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means, with respect to a Party, any entity directly or indirectly controlled by, controlling or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means (a) ownership of fifty percent (50%) (or such lesser percentage which is the maximum allowed to be owned by a foreign entity or investor in a particular jurisdiction) or more of the outstanding voting stock or other ownership interest of an entity, or (b) possession of the power to (i) elect, appoint, direct or remove fifty percent (50%) or more of the members of the board of directors or other governing body of an entity or (ii) otherwise direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of an entity by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means, with respect to a Party, any entity directly or indirectly controlled by, controlling or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means (a) ownership of fifty percent (50%) (or such lesser percentage which is the maximum allowed to be owned by a foreign entity or investor in a particular jurisdiction) or more of the outstanding voting stock or other ownership interest of an entity, or (b) possession of the power to (i) elect, appoint, direct or remove fifty percent (50%) or more of the members of the board of directors or other governing body of an entity or (ii) otherwise direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of an entity by contract or otherwise. Notwithstanding the foregoing, for purposes of Section 3.2, an 'Affiliate' shall mean only entities controlled through ownership of one hundred percent (100%) of the outstanding voting stock.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the definition of 'Affiliate'. In Section 1.1, it is defined generally, while in Section 3.2 it applies a stricter standard for its applicability within that section. This could create ambiguity as to which definition of Affiliate applies in other sections of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Lead Scaffold Payments. For each Lead Scaffold selected by Anixa, Anixa will pay OntoChem an annual fee of 10,000 U.S. Dollars, payable within thirty (30) days following each anniversary of the date of the Selection Notice, until five (5) years after the first commercial sale of the first product incorporating a compound from such Lead Scaffold, subject to Section 4.3 with respect to any Terminated Scaffold (as defined below).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Affiliate' specifically for Section 3.2, creating a stricter standard (100% ownership) than the general definition in Section 1.1 (50% ownership). This directly contradicts the general definition when Section 3.2 is interpreted in conjunction with the overall contract, creating ambiguity about which definition should apply in Section 3.2's context regarding 'Lead Scaffold Payments'. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction, as described by the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6.4 License Grant. OntoChem hereby grants to Anixa a non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, transferable, worldwide license (with the right to grant and authorize sublicenses through multiple tiers) under any patents which OntoChem or any of its Affiliates own or control during the term of this Agreement, to make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale and import the Lead Scaffold(s) and products that incorporate compounds from the Lead Scaffold(s). OntoChem will not incorporate any invention, discovery or other proprietary information owned by any third party into any Anixa Inventions or Deliverables without Anixa's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.4 License Grant. OntoChem hereby grants to Anixa a non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free, perpetual, revocable, transferable, worldwide license (with the right to grant and authorize sublicenses through multiple tiers) under any patents which OntoChem or any of its Affiliates own or control during the term of this Agreement, to make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale and import the Lead Scaffold(s) and products that incorporate compounds from the Lead Scaffold(s). OntoChem will not incorporate any invention, discovery or other proprietary information owned by any third party into any Anixa Inventions or Deliverables without Anixa's prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'irrevocable' to 'revocable' in the license grant in Section 6.4 directly contradicts the nature of a perpetual license. The original text makes the license grant perpetual and irrevocable ensuring long term usage. By making it revocable creates an in-text contradiction, where the license is stated to be long term however it can be taken away anytime during that period creating uncertainty of what is defined in the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.4 License Grant. OntoChem hereby grants to Anixa a non-exclusive, fully paid-up, royalty-free, perpetual, revocable, transferable, worldwide license (with the right to grant and authorize sublicenses through multiple tiers) under any patents which OntoChem or any of its Affiliates own or control during the term of this Agreement, to make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale and import the Lead Scaffold(s) and products that incorporate compounds from the Lead Scaffold(s). OntoChem will not incorporate any invention, discovery or other proprietary information owned by any third party into any Anixa Inventions or Deliverables without Anixa's prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'irrevocable' to 'revocable' in Section 6.4 directly contradicts the term 'perpetual' also in Section 6.4. A perpetual license, by definition, is intended to last forever and cannot be revoked. Making the license revocable introduces a fundamental inconsistency, as it creates a perpetual license that can be terminated at will, rendering the term 'perpetual' meaningless and creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration and enforceability. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.
